title: FDA Flags High-Risk Recall For Tandem Diabetes Mobi Pumps
author: Vandana Singh
description: The FDA has classified a Class I recall for Tandem Diabetes Care's Mobi insulin pumps due to a potential 'false motor failure' detection that could halt insulin delivery. Affected devices running software versions 7.6.0.1, 7.6.0.3, and 7.7.0.1 must be updated to version 7.9.0.2. As of November 4, 2025, four serious injuries have been reported with no deaths. Users are advised to have backup insulin delivery methods ready.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company faces a Class I FDA recall, the most serious designation, affecting its Mobi insulin pumps. The issue poses risks of serious injury or death if not addressed, with four reported serious injuries already documented. This creates significant regulatory, reputational, and potential financial liability risks. Stock was down 1.89% at time of publication.

title: Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. Investigated by the Portnoy Law Firm
author: Portnoy Law Firm
description: The Portnoy Law Firm has initiated a securities fraud investigation into Tandem Diabetes Care following a 19.9% stock price decline on August 7, 2025. The drop was triggered by the company's disclosure of a malfunction in its insulin pumps that could discontinue insulin delivery and cause hyperglycemia. Tandem had notified affected customers before the public market disclosure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company disclosed a critical malfunction in insulin pumps that could result in discontinuation of insulin delivery and require hospitalization. The stock fell 19.9% following the announcement, and a securities fraud investigation has been initiated, indicating potential investor harm and regulatory concerns.

title: [Latest] Global Intravenous Infusion Pump Market Size/Share Worth USD 14.02 Billion by 2035 at a 7.6% CAGR: Custom Market Insights (Analysis, Outlook, Leaders, Report, Trends, Forecast, Segmentation, Growth Rate, Value, SWOT Analysis)
author: Custom Market Insights
description: The global intravenous infusion pump market was valued at USD 6.72 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 14.02 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 7.6%. Growth is driven by increasing chronic diseases, aging populations, technological advancements in smart pumps, and rising demand for precise medication delivery in hospitals and home care settings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key market player in the growing intravenous infusion pump market, which is expanding at 7.6% CAGR with strong demand drivers from chronic disease management and technological advancement.
Identified as a prominent player in a market projected to double in size from 2025 to 2035, benefiting from increased demand for infusion therapies and home care solutions.
Highlighted as a major player with recent FDA clearance for new infusion pump products (Plum Solo and Plum Duo) in April 2025, demonstrating innovation and market expansion.
Listed as a key market player in the growing infusion pump sector, which is benefiting from technological advancements and increased healthcare investments globally.
Listed among key players in a market segment (insulin infusion pumps) experiencing growth due to rising diabetes prevalence and demand for advanced delivery systems.
Positioned as a key player in the growing infusion pump market, benefiting from increased demand for hospital and home care infusion solutions.

title: Tandem Diabetes Shares Rise 33% On Earnings Beat, Margin Expansion, Growth Outlook
author: Vandana Singh
description: Tandem Diabetes Care (NASDAQ: TNDM) shares surged 33-37% following strong Q4 earnings results that beat expectations with adjusted loss of $0.01 vs. consensus loss of $0.08, and sales of $290.4M exceeding the $277M consensus. The company reported significant margin expansion with adjusted gross profit jumping from 51% to 58%, and adjusted operating profit of $8.3M versus a $30.2M loss year-ago. For 2026, the company forecasts sales of $1.065-$1.085B and expects 5-6% adjusted EBITDA margins, though slightly below consensus expectations. Management highlighted plans for double-digit pump shipment growth and a new pay-as-you-go model to improve profitability.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong earnings beat on both EPS and revenue, significant margin expansion (gross profit 51% to 58%), positive operating profit swing, optimistic management guidance for double-digit growth, and bullish technical positioning with stock up 33-37% and trading above key moving averages. Mixed technical signals (neutral RSI but bearish MACD) are outweighed by fundamental strength and analyst Buy rating.

title: TNDM Investor News: If You Have Suffered Losses in Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. (NASDAQ: TNDM), You Are Encouraged to Contact The Rosen Law Firm About Your Rights
author: Rosen Law Firm
description: The Rosen Law Firm is investigating potential securities claims on behalf of Tandem Diabetes Care shareholders following the company's August 7, 2025 announcement of a voluntary medical device correction for select t:slim X2 insulin pumps due to a speaker-related issue that could discontinue insulin delivery. The stock fell 19.9% on the news. The firm is preparing a class action lawsuit to recover investor losses.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company issued a voluntary medical device correction for a critical product (insulin pump) that can cause discontinuation of insulin delivery, a serious safety issue. The stock declined 19.9% on the announcement, and the company is now facing a securities class action lawsuit alleging materially misleading business information was provided to investors.
